label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. Jane was born on the last day of February 2000. Today is her 16th birthday. I need to find the date one week from today in MM/DD/YYYY format. Let me break this down step by step. First, the last day of February is the 29th, right? Because February usually has 28 days, but in a leap year, it has 29. Since 2000 is a leap year (divisible by 400), so yes, February 29, 2000, is a valid date. So Jane's birthday is on 02/29/2000. Now, today is her 16th birthday. That would be 16 years later. So adding 16 years to 2000 gives 2016. So her 16th birthday is on 02/29/2016. But wait, wait. Wait, 2016 is a leap year as well, right? Because 2016 is divisible by 4 (2016 ÷ 4 = 504), so yes, February 2016 had 29 days. So today's date is 02/29/2016. Now, the question is asking for the date one week from today. So adding 7 days to 02/29/2016. Let me figure that out. February 29, 2016 plus 7 days. Let me think. February has 29 days in 2016, so after February 29th comes March 1st. So adding 7 days: Start with 02/29/2016 plus 1 day is March 1st. Then adding 6 more days. March has 31 days, so 1 + 6 = 7. So March 7th, 2016.
